challenges.prompt: Complete the closing tag so this becomes one valid paragraph element from the lesson.
challenges.codeTemplate: HTML is the skeleton of the web.<{{blank}}p>
challenges.explanation:
A closing tag reuses the same keyword as the opening tag but is prefixed with a slash, like

. The slash is what tells the browser the element is ending rather than a new one beginning.

challenges.prompt: This code has a bug — what's wrong?
options:
The inner element is closed after the outer

, so the elements are improperly nested.
The href attribute must not use quotation marks around the URL.

A paragraph is not allowed to contain a link, so cannot go inside

.
challenges.explanation:
When nesting elements you must always close the inner element before the outer one. Here the should close () before the paragraph closes (

). Anchors are perfectly fine inside paragraphs, and quoting the URL is correct.

challenges.prompt: This code has a bug — what's wrong?
challenges.code:
Hello, world!

This is my first web page.
options:
The

heading should not appear before the

paragraph.

The

element is never closed — it is missing its

closing tag.
The <body> tag needs a lang attribute like the <html> tag does.
challenges.explanation:
Most elements need both an opening and a closing tag, and forgetting the closing tag is one of the most common beginner mistakes. The paragraph opens with

but never closes with

. Heading-before-paragraph order is fine, and lang belongs on , not .

challenges.prompt: Arrange these lines into the minimal HTML document skeleton taught in the lesson.
challenges.explanation: Every HTML page starts with <!DOCTYPE html>, then the <html> root wraps everything. The <head> (invisible metadata like the <title>) comes before the <body> (the visible content), and each element closes in reverse order of how it opened.
